failureClusters.title: Unsupported rollout recommendations
failureClusters.evidence: Baseline outputs often turn partial progress signals into broad rollout or publication recommendations.

failureClusters.title: Weak citation selection
failureClusters.evidence: Outputs cite broadly relevant sources but miss the snippet that contains the actual gate, caveat, or constraint.

failureClusters.title: Cost and latency tradeoff too vague
failureClusters.evidence: Recommendations mention cost or speed without tying the decision to the 1.15x guardrail, retry caps, or progress UX.

featuredCase.title: Release note drift briefing
featuredCase.sourceEvidence: The generated notes covered 83% of merged changes, but 14% of entries described behavior that changed after final QA.
featuredCase.baseline: Publish generated release notes after a lightweight spot review because coverage is high and customers prefer concise notes.
featuredCase.candidate: Gate generated notes on drift review for changed or removed claims, and require explicit approval for sensitive customer-facing statements.
featuredCase.evaluatorNote: Candidate keeps the coverage benefit while grounding the shipping gate in stale-source drift and compliance evidence.

recommendation.label: ready for runtime
recommendation.text: Use the expanded synthetic set as the default demo eval surface before adding live LLM generation and lab-owned run orchestration.
recommendation.warning: Cost remains inside guardrail, but the next runtime slice should keep retry caps and claim-level trace summaries visible.
